channel.title: dotnet/winforms Issues
channel.description: Issues from https://github.com/dotnet/winforms, generated by https://github.com/meziantou/IssuesToRss/

item.title: Issue: How reference just single assembly from WinForms profile - @kant2002
item.description: I would like to reference just Accessibility.dll without hardcoding path to it in the project. How that's possible ?

item.title: Issue: ErrorProvider's RightToLeft display bug. - @kelicto
item.description:
When on the end of message contains any symbol, It must happend.

Reference:

item.title: Issue: Items disappear from the ListView when the user scrolls up/down - @SergeySmirnov-Akvelon
item.description:
.NET Core Version:

  • 5.0

Have you experienced this same bug with .NET Framework?:

  • No

Repro steps:

  1. Create a ListView in virtual mode with 1 million items (or use NET5 project from VirtualListView.zip)
  2. Start scrolling up/down

Actual behavior: Items disappear:

Expected behavior: Items should be displayed

item.title: Issue: Unable to find an entry point named 'AdjustWindowRectEx ForDpi' in DLL user32.dll - @dreddy-work
item.description:
Recent change for HDPI in .NET 6.0 uses latest API AdjustWindowRectEx ForDpi() from windows that was introduced in Windows 10 version >1607. We were not check for the API availability and thus running the winforms on Windows 7 is throwing Unable to find an entry point.

Fix is to make sure we call this API only on available OS and in other case, default to legacy API.

Customer report: https://developercommunity.visualstudio.com/t/unable-to-find-an-entry-point-named-Adj/1476945?entry=problem&space=61&q=AdjustWindowRectEx&viewtype=all

item.title: Issue: .net6 pre 6 exception "unable to find an entry point named ‘AdjustWindowRectExForDpi’ in DLL user32.dll" - @zxbe
item.description:
• .NET Core Version:

6.0 Preview6

  • Have you experienced this same bug with .NET Framework?:

No

Problem description:

When I Update the sdk from .net6 p 5 to p6 and run the winformapp in win7sp1 OS,I got the exception “unable to find an entry point named ‘AdjustWindowRectExForDpi’ in DLL user32.dll”.

Expected behavior:

winos: win7 ps1 x64 sdk: .NET SDK 6.0.100-preview.6.21355.2 app: winform app ide: vs2022

  1. New WinformApp
  2. Add panel Control and set Dock to Top
  3. Add label Control and set Font Bold to true
  4. Build App
  5. Copy Debug folder to Win7 Sp1 OS
  6. Click and Run the exe file
  7. Get the Exception

Minimal repro:

item.title: Issue: Some letters are cropped in a textbox when Cascadia Code font is used - @ulex
item.description:
• .NET Core Version: 5.0.301
  • Have you experienced this same bug with .NET Framework?: Yes

Problem description:

I'm observing cropping for some letters in WinForms textbox when Cascadia Code font is used. It looks like baseline is calculated incorrectly, but I cannot be sure. Compare the appearance of letters g,q and j with WPF renderer.

Expected behavior: The letters are rendered slightly higher, as in WPF renderer (although i'm not sure about umlauts)

Minimal repro:

  1. Download and install Cascadia Code font from https://github.com/microsoft/cascadia-code/releases
  2. Minimal application with WPF and WinForms versions cascadiacode_sample.zip (7kb). Build and run cascadia_sample.csproj .

item.title: Issue: [Loc]With net6 preview 7 installed on locale OS, some template names are unlocalized after runing "dotnet new --list" in CLI . - @vdanche
item.description:
1. Install net 6 preview 7 SDK[6.0.100-preview.7.21364.30(runtime:6.0.0-preview.7.21360.10)] from https://github.com/dotnet/installer
  2. Install it on locale OS
  3. Run “dotnet new --list” in CLI
  4. Check if output contents are localized well. Actual Result: Some template names are not localized.

e.g SC:

DE:

Note: It has been localized well in VS 17.0 preview 3 e,g SC:

item.title: Issue: ClickOnce user.config update does not work on .Net Core runtime - @lGSMl
item.description:
Description

We have recently moved our clickonce app on .net core 3.1, as .net 5 SDK now supports publishing clickonce apps.

After moving to core we faced an issue that user.config is not preserved anymore between application updates.

According to this clickonce documentation - user.config in the new version should be merged with an existing one. That was the case with 4.8, but it does not work on core.

Configuration

Core 3.1, win10-x64

Regression?

Regression from framework 4.8

Other information

After some debugging I was able to figure out that on core runtime there is a snippet missing in comparison to 4.8:

How path to data folder is resolved in core How path to data folder is resolved in 4.8

So this part is missing on core:

if (this.IsClickOnceDeployed(AppDomain.CurrentDomain)) {
            string dataPath = AppDomain.CurrentDomain.GetData(ClickOnceDataDirectory) as string;
            string versionSuffix = Validate(_productVersion, false);

            // NOTE: No roaming config for clickonce - not supported.
            if (Path.IsPathRooted(dataPath)) {
                _localConfigDirectory = CombineIfValid(dataPath, versionSuffix);
                _localConfigFilename  = CombineIfValid(_localConfigDirectory, UserConfigFilename);
            }

        }

That leads to creation of new data folder on every app update via clickonce, and, presumably, causes issue with clickonce user.config update.

item.title: Issue: Narrator can't announce the full information when go through the RadioButton control by using Up/Down/Left/Right arrow - @Zheng-Li01
item.description:
• .NET Core Version: .NET 6.0.100-preview.7.21363.2

  • Have you experienced this same bug with .NET Framework?: No

Problem description: Narrator can't announce the full information when go through the RadioButton control by using Up/Down/Left/Right arrow, which is regression issue, can't reproduce on .NET Core 3.1.

Expected behavior: Narrator can announce the full information when go through the RadioButton control by using Up/Down/Left/Right arrow.

Actual behavior: Narrator can't announce the full information when go through the RadioButton control by using Up/Down/Left/Right arrow.

Minimal repro:

  1. Open the attached application. Application_Issue2.zip
  2. Build and run it.
  3. Go through the RadioButton control by using Up/Down/Left/Right arrow.
  4. Open the Narrator tool, and observe its behavior.

item.title: Issue: The list items displaying with incorrect order under the list tree node in Inspect when expanding the ImageAlign/TextAlign/Dock properties comboBox in PropertyGrid - @Zheng-Li01
item.description:
• .NET Core Version: .NET 6.0.100-preview.7.21363.2
  • Have you experienced this same bug with .NET Framework?: No, the feature is no supported in the .NET Framework & .NET Core 3.1, see details in the GH issue 3027

Problem description: The list items displaying with incorrect order under the list tree node in Inspect when expanding the ImageAlign/TextAlign/Dock properties comboBox in PropertyGrid.

Expected behavior: The list items displaying with correct order under the list tree node in Inspect when expanding the ImageAlign/TextAlign/Dock properties comboBox in PropertyGrid.

Actual behavior: The list items displaying with incorrect order under the list tree node in Inspect when expanding the ImageAlign/TextAlign/Dock properties comboBox in PropertyGrid.

Minimal repro:

  1. Open the attached application. WinFormsApp_Issue1.zip
  2. Build and run it.
  3. Expanding the ImageAlign/TextAlign/Dock properties comboBox in PropertyGrid.
  4. Open the Inspect tool, and observe the list items displaying order under the list tree node in Inspect.

More info:

  1. The issue also can reproduce .NET Core 5.0, can't reproduce .NET Core 3.1.
  2. The issue can't reproduce others properties, such as BackColor/ForeColor.
  3. The issue also can reproduce AccessibilityInsigt tool.

item.title: Issue: WinForm Core 5/6 Designer on Visual Studio 2019.10 100% scale mode in 4K display. - @IrakliLomidze
item.description:
• .NET Core Version: 5, 6-Prev5
  • Have you experienced this same bug with .NET Framework?:

Problem description:

.NET Framework Windows Forms is working correctly. I tried VS 2019.10 is VM with 96DPI screen mode and Winform designers on autoscale mode works more or less acceptable.

Please Fix that Bug in VS 100% scale core designers.

item.title: Issue: TabControl difficult to support Dark mode - @docfresh
item.description:
It seems WinForms on .NET 5 has no built-in support for dark mode theming. I can implement a custom darkmode theme switcher that sets BackColor and ForeColor on most controls easily, but TabControl doesn't support it. There is no built in way to override the paint event without building a custom control that inherits TabControl and overrides OnPaint/Draw. TabControl also seems unresponsive to the Win32 API SetWindowsTheme(). It would be wonderful if some properties were exposed to set BackColor and ForeColor on TabControl, and maybe some ways to draw the border of a selected tab, especially when Appearance = FlatButtons, to make it easier to theme "darkly". Or if Metro style tabcontrols were available for Winforms.

SetWindowTheme(tabInfo.Handle, "DarkMode_Explorer", null); [uxtheme.dll]

item.title: Issue: Unit tests success depends on user settings - @OlegRa
item.description:
• .NET Core Version: 6.0.100-preview.6.21317.3

  • Have you experienced this same bug with .NET Framework?: N/A

Problem description:

Many unit tests that check the Control.ShowKeyboardCues and Control.ShowFocusCues default values for newly created controls failed on Windows 10 box with the enabled "Underline access keys when available" option.

Expected behavior:

Unit tests shouldn't depend on user/computer settings. At least they should use these settings for calculating expected values.

Minimal repro:

Run unit tests on PC with the "Underline access keys when available" option enabled.

item.title: Issue: When is expected a stable version of WinForm core Designer ? - @IrakliLomidze
item.description:
When is expected a stable version of WinForm core Designer?

.NET 5 Designer was not updated for while.

item.title: Issue: WinForms HighDPI PerMonitorV2 RichTextBox looses formatting - @Balkoth
item.description:
.NET Core Version: SDK: 5.0.301 (ef17233f86) Runtime: 5.0.7 (556582d964)

Problem description: Moving a window containing a RichTextBox between two displays makes the RichTextBox drop all formattings when PerMonitorV2 is enabled.

Starting on main display:

After being moved to second display:

Minimal repro: A sample is attached here: TestDpiRichTextBox.zip
